core_mechanism: harmonic (Fourier) decomposition of an object over an orthogonal basis graded by 'degree/level', governed by a hypercontractive smoothing-operator inequality that bounds the growth of higher L^p norms in terms of lower ones and thereby forces the spectrum to be concentrated on low degrees
brief_reason: The abstract builds its whole method on the Fourier spectra of these functions (large-coefficient recovery, spectral-concentration theorems) and explicitly proves a hypercontractive inequality of the Bonami-Gross-Beckner type to control that spectrum, which is the transferable analytic engine.
